how_it_works: The PoE passive skill tree has 7 starting positions, one per character class. Each starting area clusters nodes matching that class's primary archetype, but all areas of the tree are reachable by any class — it just costs more passive points the further you travel. Choosing the right starting class (and by extension, starting area) is the most impactful decision when building a new character.
tips: Distance on the passive tree costs passive points — start near your core cluster to maximize efficiency
Scion starts in the center and can efficiently reach any area, but sacrifices depth for breadth
Several powerful keystones are class-adjacent — e.g. Resolute Technique (Duelist), Mind Over Matter (Witch)
Ascendancy class choices within each starting area often matter more than the starting position itself
Efficiency matters most at high point investment — builds spending 90+ passives feel the travel cost sharply

avoid_for: Spellcasters (too far from spell damage nodes)
Energy Shield builds (no ES nodes nearby)
starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Juggernaut: best league starter and tankiest class; Chieftain: RF and fire builds; Berserker: maximum offence with risk.
starting_areas.notes: One of the most beginner-friendly starting areas. Life and armour nodes are abundant and self-explanatory. Juggernaut Marauder is perennially the #1 league-starter recommendation.

starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Gladiator: spell block and bleed immunity; Champion: permanent Fortify and impale; Slayer: culling strike and leech immunity.
starting_areas.notes: Excellent for attack-based builds. Resolute Technique nearby removes the need to invest in accuracy. Gladiator's block mechanic is one of the best defences in the game.

recommended_for: Bow builds (Tornado Shot, Lightning Arrow, Explosive Arrow)
avoid_for: Life-heavy builds (few life nodes near Ranger start)
starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Pathfinder: flask automation and poison; Raider: frenzy charges and phasing; Deadeye: projectile damage and Tailwind.
starting_areas.notes: Best starting point for bow and evasion builds. Acrobatics nearby is a huge defensive option. Pathfinder is one of the strongest ascendancies in the game for poison/flask builds.

avoid_for: Life builds requiring heavy investment (fewer life nodes)
starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Assassin: critical multiplier and poison; Saboteur: trap/mine damage and ignite prolif; Trickster: soul drinker and dodge.
starting_areas.notes: The critical strike hub of the tree. Shadow has access to both spell and attack crit nodes, making it flexible. Saboteur is the go-to for trap/mine builds.

recommended_for: Minion builds (Necromancer SRS, Raise Spectre, Animate Weapon)
avoid_for: Life-based melee builds (very far from life/melee nodes)
starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Necromancer: best minion ascendancy by far; Occultist: ES recovery and curses; Elementalist: elemental prolif and golems.
starting_areas.notes: The caster and summoner hub. Necromancer is consistently the strongest summoner ascendancy. Occultist pairs well with CI for zero-cost Eldritch Battery setups.

avoid_for: Pure evasion builds (no evasion nodes nearby)
Builds relying on pure chaos damage
starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Inquisitor: consecrated ground and elemental crit; Hierophant: mana/totem; Guardian: auras and minion support.
starting_areas.notes: The hybrid caster/support area. Guardian is the definitive aura-bot ascendancy in group play. Inquisitor is popular for elemental conversion builds due to its penetration.

recommended_for: Builds requiring nodes from 3+ different class areas
Dual-ascendancy benefit builds (Ascendant picks two sub-ascendancies)
avoid_for: New players (Ascendant is complex to optimize)
Builds that only need nodes in one class area (any other class is more efficient)
notable_keystones_nearby: None specifically — equidistant to all keystones
starting_areas.ascendancy_highlights: Ascendant: choose any two class-ascendancy nodes from other classes. Powerful for hybrids like Jugg+Raider or Slayer+Pathfinder.
starting_areas.notes: Scion's power comes from Ascendant's dual-subclass system. She is weaker than specialized classes in most meta builds, but uniquely flexible for hybrid strategies.
